{
  "cefr_level": "B1",
  "level_basis": {
    "textinspector_level_raw": null,
    "flesch_kincaid": null,
    "rule_notes": "Estimated holistically: vocabulary shows B1+ range ('prioritize', 'productivity', 'task-management'), but grammar control remains at B1 with consistent article errors, embedded question word order issues, and spelling errors.   },
  "communicative_task_achievement": "Yes",
  "communicative_task_notes": [
    "Addresses the Telegram post directly with empathy ('you are not alone').",
    "Provides four relevant tips on time management and organization.",
    "Meets length requirement (12 sentences; task asked for 10)."
  ],
  "grammar_score": 0.72,
  "corrections": [
    "Hellow -> Hello (Lexis; A1; Low)",
    "me to -> me too (Lexis; A1; Low)",
    "In first semester -> In the first semester (Grammar; A2; Low)",
    "wery -> very (Lexis; A1; Low)",
    "it was wery big problem -> it was a very big problem (Grammar; A2; Low)",
    "how can I fix -> how I can fix (Word order; B1; Medium)",
    "I will give you them -> I will give them to you (Word order; A2; Low)",
    "some task-management system -> a task-management system (Grammar; A2; Low)",
    "in uni -> at university (Register; B1; Low)",
    "enouth -> enough (Lexis; A1; Low)",
    "bust -> boost (Lexis; B1; Medium)"
  ],
  "errors_by_category": {
    "Lexis": [
      {"excerpt": "Hellow", "correction": "Hello", "comment": "Spelling error; basic greeting word.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A1"},
      {"excerpt": "me to", "correction": "me too", "comment": "Homophone confusion; 'too' = 'also'.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A1"},
      {"excerpt": "wery", "correction": "very", "comment": "Spelling error; common adverb.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A1"},
      {"excerpt": "enouth", "correction": "enough", "comment": "Spelling error; common quantifier.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A1"},
      {"excerpt": "bust", "correction": "boost", "comment": "Wrong word; 'bust' means to break, 'boost' means to increase.", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "B1"}
    ],
    "Grammar": [
      {"excerpt": "In first semester", "correction": "In the first semester", "comment": "Missing definite article before specific time reference.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A2"},
      {"excerpt": "it was wery big problem", "correction": "it was a very big problem", "comment": "Missing indefinite article before singular count noun.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A2"},
      {"excerpt": "some task-management system", "correction": "a task-management system", "comment": "Article choice; 'some' is vague here, 'a' is more natural for introducing a concept.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A2"}
    ],
    "Punctuation": [],
    "Word order": [
      {"excerpt": "how can I fix", "correction": "how I can fix", "comment": "Embedded question requires subject-verb inversion removal.", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "B1"},
      {"excerpt": "I will give you them", "correction": "I will give them to you", "comment": "Pronoun placement after 'give' requires indirect object before direct object, or 'to' phrase.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A2"}
    ],
    "Sentence structure": [],
    "Text organization": [],
    "Discourse markers": [],
    "Rhetorical features": [],
    "Register": [
      {"excerpt": "in uni", "correction": "at university", "comment": "Informal abbreviation 'uni' acceptable in chat context but noted for awareness.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "B1"}
    ],
    "Incomprehensibility": []
  },
  "strengths": [
    {"item": "prioritize", "cefr_level": "B2", "category": "Vocabulary", "note": "B2-level verb used correctly in context."},
    {"item": "task-management system", "cefr_level": "B2", "category": "Vocabulary", "note": "Sophisticated compound noun phrase; shows lexical range."},
    {"item": "productivity", "cefr_level": "B2", "category": "Vocabulary", "note": "Abstract noun appropriate for advice context."},
    {"item": "Combine similar tasks", "cefr_level": "B2", "category": "Vocabulary", "note": "Good collocation and practical advice phrasing."},
    {"item": "Numbered list organization", "cefr_level": "B1", "category": "Discourse marker", "note": "Clear structure for presenting multiple tips; appropriate for task type."}
  ],
  "feedback": "CEFR Level: B1. This response successfully addresses the task with relevant, practical advice. Vocabulary shows ambition beyond B1 (prioritize, productivity, task-management system), but grammar control remains at B1 level with consistent article errors, embedded question word order issues, and several spelling errors. The numbered list structure effectively organizes the tips. Strengths include B2-level vocabulary items and clear text organization. Main areas for improvement: embedded question word order (how I can fix, not how can I fix), article use with singular count nouns, and careful proofreading for spelling.",
  "next_steps": [
    "Practice embedded questions: after 'ideas about', 'know', 'wonder', use statement word order (e.g., 'I have ideas how I can fix it').",
    "Review article rules for singular count nouns: 'a problem', 'the first semester', 'a system'.",
    "Proofread for common spelling patterns: 'enough' (not 'enouth'), 'very' (not 'wery'), 'boost' (not 'bust')."
  ]
}

{
  "cefr_level": "B1",
  "level_basis": {
    "textinspector_level_raw": null,

    "rule_notes": "Estimated holistically: vocabulary range (concentrate, productive, satisfied, process) and sentence variety indicate B1; grammar errors (demonstrative agreement, articles, conditional) are consistent with B1 level; cohesion markers present but limited range."
  },
  "communicative_task_achievement": "Yes",
  "communicative_task_notes": ["Addresses the Telegram post directly with empathy and advice.", "Provides two clear tips (TODO list and breaks) with explanation.", "Sentence count approximately 12, meeting the 10-sentence requirement."],
  "grammar_score": 0.72,
  "corrections": [
    "I think, that -> I think that (Punctuation; B1; Low)",
    "Also you can mark -> Also, you can mark (Punctuation; B1; Low)",
    "the list like this -> a list like this (Grammar; A2; Low)",
    "the smaller ones -> smaller ones (Grammar; B1; Low)",
    "this tips -> these tips (Grammar; A2; Medium)",
    "would't -> won't (Grammar; B1; Medium)",
    "TODO -> to-do (Punctuation; B1; Low)"
  ],
  "errors_by_category": {
    "Lexis": [],
    "Grammar": [
      {"excerpt": "the list like this", "correction": "a list like this", "comment": "Indefinite article needed for first mention; definite article implies specific list already known.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A2"},
      {"excerpt": "the smaller ones", "correction": "smaller ones", "comment": "Definite article unnecessary here; 'smaller ones' is non-specific.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "B1"},
      {"excerpt": "this tips", "correction": "these tips", "comment": "Demonstrative agreement error: 'tips' is plural, requires 'these'.", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "A2"},
      {"excerpt": "would't", "correction": "won't", "comment": "Spelling error and conditional mismatch: first conditional requires 'will/won't', not 'would'.", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "B1"}
    ],
    "Punctuation": [
      {"excerpt": "I think, that", "correction": "I think that", "comment": "Comma not used before 'that' in English that-clauses.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "B1"},
      {"excerpt": "Also you can mark", "correction": "Also, you can mark", "comment": "Sentence-initial adverb 'Also' should be followed by a comma.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "B1"},
      {"excerpt": "TODO", "correction": "to-do", "comment": "Standard spelling is 'to-do list' with hyphen; TODO is acceptable in informal digital contexts but non-standard.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "B1"}
    ],
    "Word order": [],
    "Sentence structure": [],
    "Text organization": [],
    "Discourse markers": [],
    "Rhetorical features": [],
    "Register": [],
    "Incomprehensibility": []
  },
  "strengths": [
    {"item": "I had (and sometimes have) this feeling too", "cefr_level": "B2", "category": "Grammar", "note": "Parenthetical insertion shows syntactic flexibility above B1."},
    {"item": "the studying process", "cefr_level": "B2", "category": "Vocabulary", "note": "Abstract noun phrase with 'process' shows lexical precision."},
    {"item": "split them into", "cefr_level": "B2", "category": "Vocabulary", "note": "Phrasal verb 'split into' used accurately."},
    {"item": "productive and satisfied", "cefr_level": "B2", "category": "Vocabulary", "note": "Sophisticated adjective collocation for expressing outcome."},
    {"item": "Firstly", "cefr_level": "B1", "category": "Discourse marker", "note": "Appropriate sequencing marker for organizing tips."}
  ],
  "feedback": "This response demonstrates B1-level writing with clear task achievement. The text uses appropriate vocabulary (concentrate, productive, satisfied) and organizes ideas with discourse markers (Firstly, Also, But). Grammar errors are present but do not impede communication: demonstrative agreement ('this tips'), article use, and conditional structure need attention. Strengths above B1 include parenthetical structure and phrasal verbs. To progress to B2, focus on grammatical accuracy in plural demonstratives, article usage, and conditional sentences.",
  "next_steps": ["Practice demonstrative agreement: this/that + singular, these/those + plural nouns.", "Review first conditional structure: If + present, will/won't + base verb.", "Study article usage with abstract nouns and first vs. subsequent mentions."]
}

  "corrections": [
    "Everyone got -> Everyone has (Grammar; A2; Medium)",
    "same time a day -> the same amount of time in a day (Grammar; B1; Medium)",
    "study good -> study well (Grammar; A2; Medium)",
    "deadlines to be done -> deadlines you have (Lexis; B1; Low)",
    "homeworks -> homework assignments (Grammar; A2; Medium)",
    "Also Its important -> Also, it's important (Punctuation; B1; Low)",
    "If subject seems -> If a subject seems (Grammar; A2; Medium)",
    "intresting -> interesting (Punctuation; A2; Low)",
    "its not needed -> it's not needed (Grammar; A2; Medium)",
    "next courses -> future courses (Lexis; B1; Low)",
    "max grade -> the maximum grade (Grammar; B1; Low)",
    "organise your days, set priorities -> organise your days and set priorities (Sentence structure; B1; Medium)",
    "keep in mind, that -> keep in mind that (Punctuation; B1; Low)"
  ],
  "errors_by_category": {
    "Lexis": [
      {"excerpt": "deadlines to be done", "correction": "deadlines you have", "comment": "Deadlines are not 'done'; awkward phrasing. Better: 'deadlines you have' or simply 'deadlines'.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "B1"},
      {"excerpt": "next courses", "correction": "future courses", "comment": "'Next courses' is unnatural; 'future courses' or 'subsequent courses' is more appropriate.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "B1"}
    ],
    "Grammar": [
      {"excerpt": "Everyone got", "correction": "Everyone has", "comment": "Present simple 'has' is needed for general truth; 'got' is past tense or informal.", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "A2"},
      {"excerpt": "same time a day", "correction": "the same amount of time in a day", "comment": "Missing article 'the' and awkward phrasing. Should be 'the same amount of time in a day' or 'the same time each day'.", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "B1"},
      {"excerpt": "study good", "correction": "study well", "comment": "Adverb 'well' needed after verb 'study', not adjective 'good'.", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "A2"},
      {"excerpt": "homeworks", "correction": "homework assignments", "comment": "'Homework' is uncountable; cannot add -s. Use 'homework' or 'homework assignments'.", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "A2"},
      {"excerpt": "Its important", "correction": "It's important", "comment": "Contraction 'it's' (it is) needed, not possessive 'its'.", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "A2"},
      {"excerpt": "If subject seems", "correction": "If a subject seems", "comment": "Missing indefinite article before singular countable noun 'subject'.", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "A2"},
      {"excerpt": "its not needed", "correction": "it's not needed", "comment": "Contraction 'it's' (it is) needed, not possessive 'its'.", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "A2"},
      {"excerpt": "max grade", "correction": "the maximum grade", "comment": "Missing article and informal abbreviation; 'the maximum grade' is more appropriate.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "B1"}
    ],
    "Punctuation": [
      {"excerpt": "Also Its", "correction": "Also, it's", "comment": "Comma needed after 'Also' at sentence start; apostrophe needed in 'it's'.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "B1"},
      {"excerpt": "intresting", "correction": "interesting", "comment": "Spelling error: missing 'e' after 't'.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A2"},
      {"excerpt": "keep in mind, that", "correction": "keep in mind that", "comment": "Unnecessary comma before 'that' clause.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "B1"}
    ],
    "Word order": [],
    "Sentence structure": [
      {"excerpt": "you have to organise your days, set priorities", "correction": "you have to organise your days and set priorities", "comment": "Comma splice: two verb phrases joined only by comma. Use 'and' or restructure.", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "B1"}
    ],
    "Text organization": [],
    "Discourse markers": [],
    "Rhetorical features": [],
    "Register": [],
    "Incomprehensibility": []
  },
  "strengths": [

    {"item": "deadlines", "cefr_level": "B1", "category": "Vocabulary", "note": "Appropriate topic vocabulary for time management context."},
    {"item": "priorities", "cefr_level": "B2", "category": "Vocabulary", "note": "B2 noun used appropriately."},
    {"item": "exhausted", "cefr_level": "B2", "category": "Vocabulary", "note": "B2 adjective conveying emotional/physical state effectively."},
    {"item": "To sum up", "cefr_level": "B1", "category": "Discourse marker", "note": "Appropriate concluding marker for structuring the response."},
    {"item": "why do you need to do all the tasks and get exhausted every day to get max grade?", "cefr_level": "B2", "category": "Rhetorical feature", "note": "Effective rhetorical question engaging the reader and reinforcing the argument about prioritization."}
  ],
  "feedback": "CEFR Level: B1. The text demonstrates B1-level proficiency with some B2 vocabulary ambition ('allocate', 'priorities', 'exhausted'). Grammar control is the main limiting factor: multiple A2-B1 errors in articles ('Everyone got same time', 'If subject seems'), verb-adverb confusion ('study good'), uncountable nouns ('homeworks'), and contractions ('Its' vs 'it's'). Sentence structure is generally clear but includes a comma splice. Communicative task is achieved with relevant, practical advice. Strengths include effective use of a rhetorical question and appropriate discourse markers. Per methodology, level set at B1 due to inconsistent grammatical accuracy despite vocabulary range.",
  "next_steps": [
    "Review uncountable nouns: 'homework', 'advice', 'information' cannot take plural -s.",
    "Practice adverb formation: adjectives like 'good' become 'well' after verbs.",
    "Consolidate article use with singular countable nouns: 'a subject', 'the same time'."
  ]
}
